name: Bipartite Segments
statement:
You are given an undirected graph with n vertices. There are no edge-simple cycles with the even length in it. In other words, there are no cycles of even length that pass each edge at most once. Let's enumerate vertices from 1 to n.
You have to answer q queries. Each query is described by a segment of vertices [l;‚ÄČr], and you have to count the number of its subsegments [x;‚ÄČy] (l‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČx‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČy‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČr), such that if we delete all vertices except the segment of vertices [x;‚ÄČy] (including x and y) and edges between them, the resulting graph is bipartite.
input:
The first line contains two integers n and m (1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČn‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČ3¬∑105, 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČm‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČ3¬∑105)¬†‚ÄĒ the number of vertices and the number of edges in the graph.
The next m lines describe edges in the graph. The i-th of these lines contains two integers ai and bi (1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČai,‚ÄČbi‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČn; ai‚ÄČ‚Č†‚ÄČbi), denoting an edge between vertices ai and bi. It is guaranteed that this graph does not contain edge-simple cycles of even length.
The next line contains a single integer q (1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČq‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČ3¬∑105)¬†‚ÄĒ the number of queries.
The next q lines contain queries. The i-th of these lines contains two integers li and ri (1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČli‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČri‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČn)¬†‚ÄĒ the query parameters.
output: Print q numbers, each in new line: the i-th of them should be the number of subsegments [x;‚ÄČy] (li‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČx‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČy‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČri), such that the graph that only includes vertices from segment [x;‚ÄČy] and edges between them is bipartite.
note:
The first example is shown on the picture below:
For the first query, all subsegments of [1;‚ÄČ3], except this segment itself, are suitable.
For the first query, all subsegments of [4;‚ÄČ6], except this segment itself, are suitable.
For the third query, all subsegments of [1;‚ÄČ6] are suitable, except [1;‚ÄČ3], [1;‚ÄČ4], [1;‚ÄČ5], [1;‚ÄČ6], [2;‚ÄČ6], [3;‚ÄČ6], [4;‚ÄČ6].
